channel.title: The QUILL Cast
channel.description: A 36-episode, two-host audio course on QUILL, the free, screen-reader-first writing studio from Community Access. Liam and Jessica lead you from your very first launch to mastery of every feature family: the editor and keyboard, documents and formats, document rescue and OCR, the private speech suite, optional AI, the Accessible Vault, Story Studio, GLOW accessibility review, braille production, extensions, and the trust architecture underneath it all. Episode voices are generated with QUILL's own on-device Kokoro neural speech engine.
channel.itunes:author: Community Access / BITS
channel.itunes:subtitle: A complete audio course on the screen-reader-first writing studio
channel.itunes:summary: A 36-episode, two-host audio course on QUILL, the free, screen-reader-first writing studio from Community Access. Liam and Jessica lead you from your very first launch to mastery of every feature family: the editor and keyboard, documents and formats, document rescue and OCR, the private speech suite, optional AI, the Accessible Vault, Story Studio, GLOW accessibility review, braille production, extensions, and the trust architecture underneath it all. Episode voices are generated with QUILL's own on-device Kokoro neural speech engine.

item.title: 1: Welcome to QUILL
item.description: What QUILL is and who it is for: the three design promises (QUILL owns the essentials; everything optional is off by default; nothing leaves your computer without asking), why free matters, and how to follow this course. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep01-welcome-to-quill.html

item.title: 2: Install and First Launch
item.description: The installer, self-voicing setup, the wizard, and feature profiles - why smaller menus win for screen reader users. Plus the three day-one habits: F1, the optional components store, and the guaranteed Escape. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep02-install-and-first-launch.html

item.title: 3: Your First Document
item.description: Files in and out: new, open, save, recent files that respect removable drives, cursor position memory, and the silent crash-recovery net that is protecting you from day one. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep03-your-first-document.html

item.title: 4: The Main Window
item.description: The menu bar by intent, tabs, and the status bar as narrator. The Spoken Echo (Alt+Shift+E), the dialog contract that means you can never be trapped, and why nothing ever steals focus. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep04-the-main-window.html

item.title: 5: The Command Palette
item.description: Ctrl+Shift+P: reach any of hundreds of commands by typing a fragment. The palette as discovery tool, shortcut tutor, and the end of menu-path memorization. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep05-the-command-palette.html

item.title: 6: What QUILL Says
item.description: The verbosity system: profiles, Quiet and Meeting modes, status queries (Where am I? What changed?), anti-spam machinery, and the glorious 'Why did QUILL say that?' explainer. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep06-what-quill-says.html

item.title: 7: Moving Through Text
item.description: Navigation as a vocabulary: characters to headings, bookmarks that survive restarts, selection marks, Go To Line, and the QUILL key's Quick Nav and Browse Mode introduced. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep07-moving-through-text.html

item.title: 8: The QUILL Key
item.description: The prefix system in full: why chords exist, a tour of what lives behind the QUILL key, Browse Mode as the editor's virtual cursor, and how to adopt chords without memorizing a chart. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep08-the-quill-key.html

item.title: 9: Make the Keyboard Yours
item.description: The Keymap Editor: two-way search including reverse shortcut lookup, Record Keys, informed one-step conflict swaps, keymap diagnostics with one-click Heal, and shareable keyboard packs. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep09-make-the-keyboard-yours.html

item.title: 10: Editing Power Tools
item.description: Selection and marks, the undo contract (compound changes are one step), line surgery, section moves, case transforms, and the revived classics: Repeat, Restore Deleted Text, and Describe Character. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep10-editing-power-tools.html

item.title: 11: Find, Replace, Navigate
item.description: Search with spoken match counts, replace-all as one undo step, a gentle first regular expression, and Search in Files across whole folders. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep11-find-replace-navigate.html

item.title: 12: Spelling and Word Tools
item.description: Misspelling navigation (Ctrl+F7) as the fair replacement for red squiggles, the spell dialog, custom dictionaries, downloadable languages, and the dictionary and thesaurus. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep12-spelling-and-word-tools.html

item.title: 13: Never Lose Work
item.description: The full safety stack layer by layer: undo, autosave, crash recovery, backups, versions, snapshots, atomic writes, and honest failure reporting. Safety as courage. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep13-never-lose-work.html

item.title: 14: Markdown and Structure
item.description: The structure language everything builds on: the core syntax in minutes, why structure is audible here, the live preview, and the style habits that pay off across a dozen later features. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep14-markdown-and-structure.html

item.title: 15: The Text-Supply Toolkit
item.description: The twelve-slot Copy Tray, snippets with prompts and cursor markers, auto-expanding abbreviations, and recorded macros - four tools that bring text to your cursor. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep15-clipboard-snippets-macros.html

item.title: 16: Rich Formatting, Hidden Codes
item.description: Real fonts, colors, and alignment with a clean plain-text buffer: Describe Formatting at Cursor, Reveal Codes, and Illuminations - formatting that survives inside a plain .txt. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep16-rich-formatting-hidden-codes.html

item.title: 17: Word, EPUB, PDF and Friends
item.description: Other people's formats with dignity: Word round-trips with real heading styles, the EPUB navigator, honest PDF extraction quality, the import/export engine, and batch conversion. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep17-word-epub-pdf-and-friends.html

item.title: 18: Document Rescue and OCR
item.description: Free first, local first, nothing uploaded: MarkItDown for born-digital files, the scanned-PDF escalation prompt, on-device Tesseract OCR with confidence honesty, and the verified engine install. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep18-document-rescue-ocr.html

item.title: 19: Files Everywhere
item.description: FTP, SFTP, WebDAV, and S3 from three commands; GitHub editing with no Git installed; SSH editing with strict host keys; Open from URL - the end of the download-edit-reupload shuffle. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep19-files-everywhere.html

item.title: 20: Watch Folders and Automation
item.description: Teach QUILL to work while you're elsewhere: auto-opening inbox folders, watch actions and pipelines, and the safety posture that keeps automation from becoming mystery. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep20-watch-folders-automation.html

item.title: 21: Read Aloud and the Voice Catalog
item.description: SAPI, eSpeak, DECtalk (hello, Paul), on-device Kokoro neural voices, the optional cloud voice, the browser reader, and the SSML Builder - a voice for every job. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep21-read-aloud-and-voices.html

item.title: 22: Dictation
item.description: You talk, QUILL types, offline: Whisper-powered hold-to-talk and locked dictation, model choices for real hardware, and the speak-fast-repair-fast workflow. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep22-dictation.html

item.title: 23: Transcription and the Listening Companion
item.description: Recordings into documents, privately: local transcription with translation and speaker options, Transcript Actions from minutes to follow-up emails, and the no-syntax Action Builder. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep23-transcription-listening-companion.html

item.title: 24: Text to Audio, Audiobooks, DAISY
item.description: Documents out to sound: speech audio export in every format, chaptered M4B audiobooks from your heading structure, and native DAISY talking-book export for accessible libraries. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep24-text-to-audio-audiobooks-daisy.html

item.title: 25: Setting Up AI
item.description: Off by default, reviewable always, free for everyone: the setup wizard's honest choices (local Ollama, free OpenRouter key, paid accounts, or Not Now) and the privacy architecture underneath. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep25-setting-up-ai.html

item.title: 26: Ask Quill and Everyday AI
item.description: One conversation that knows your document, the quick-action toolkit, AI spell/grammar/thesaurus above the local engines, custom instructions, and the discipline of rejecting things. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep26-ask-quill-everyday-ai.html

item.title: 27: The AI Library - Prompts and Skills
item.description: A build-along up the ladder: saving your first prompt with variables, promoting it into a multi-step skill, sharing packs, and curating a bench that fits your actual week. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep27-ai-library-prompts-skills.html

item.title: 28: Agents - Reviewable Autonomy
item.description: Plans you read before they run: the plan-review-execute contract, the Accessibility Tune-Up as flagship, partial approval, and the craft of reviewing plans. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep28-agents-reviewable-autonomy.html

item.title: 29: The Accessible Vault
item.description: Linked notes rebuilt for the ear: wikilinks, the spoken 'what links here', Note Neighborhood, unlinked mentions, fearless rename - the graph view without the graph. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep29-accessible-vault-basics.html

item.title: 30: Vault Power
item.description: Tags that roll up, templates with prompts, daily notes, embeds, exporting your vault as an accessible website, and Git sync on infrastructure you own. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep30-vault-power.html

item.title: 31: Story Studio
item.description: Book-length writing with a keyboard-first binder: structure straight from your headings, character and plot-thread detail forms as front matter, and compile-to-manuscript. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep31-story-studio.html

item.title: 32: GLOW - Audit and Fix
item.description: Guided confidence, not a compliance dashboard: plain-language audits of the document in front of you, in-place selection fixes, and whole-document fixes with a before/after compare. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep32-glow-audit-and-fix.html

item.title: 33: GLOW for Files
item.description: Scored, graded audits of Word, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and EPUB; non-destructive Fix File that never touches the original; and the signature-verified engine update path. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep33-glow-for-files.html

item.title: 34: Braille Production
item.description: BRF, BRL, PEF and UEB workflows; Read Layout Metrics and the longest-line repair loop; trailing-space cleanup; and the honest cell-two display workaround story. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep34-braille-production.html

item.title: 35: Quillins and the Developer Console
item.description: The extension system: worker-process fault isolation, Python and JavaScript Quillins, the deliberately-gated marketplace, and the accessible scripting console that turns users into toolmakers. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep35-quillins-and-the-console.html

item.title: 36: Trust, Community, and the Road Ahead
item.description: The finale: the five enforced safety rules, honest diagnostics, the community and its receipts, the public roadmap, and what QUILL proves about designing screen-reader-first. Full transcript: https://community-access.github.io/quill/podcast/transcripts/ep36-trust-community-finale.html
